text: Detecting text in natural scene images is a fundamental requirement for a wide range of content-based image analysis tasks. In this paper, we present an accurate and robust framework for scene text detection. Our approach begins with a fast and effective pruning algorithm designed to extract Maximally Stable Extremal Regions (MSERs) as character candidates by minimizing regularized variations. These candidates are then grouped into potential text regions using a single-link clustering algorithm, where distance weights and clustering thresholds are automatically determined through a novel self-training distance metric learning strategy. To refine the results, we utilize a character classifier to estimate the posterior probability that a candidate represents non-text; high-probability false positives are eliminated, and the final text regions are confirmed via a dedicated text classifier. The proposed system was evaluated on the ICDAR 2011 Robust Reading Competition database, achieving an f-measure of over 76%—a significant improvement over the previous state-of-the-art of 71%. Further experiments on multilingual, street view, multi-orientation, and born-digital datasets demonstrate the versatility and effectiveness of the proposed method.
